name: Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla
name_original: Dolmen de Soto – circular desde Niebla
description: Hike a moderate 10.8-mile (17.4 km) circular route from Niebla, exploring the lands of Niebla and the ancient Dolmen de Soto.
region_intro: Hiking around Niebla offers diverse landscapes, from the distinctive red and orange waters of the Río Tinto to ancient historical sites. The region features plains, wetlands, and the impressive Almohad wall encircling the town. Trails often incorporate views of the river, historical bridges, and megalithic monuments. The terrain provides a mix of easy riverside walks and moderate loops through varied natural settings. Best hiking trails around Niebla The most popular hiking route is Fog - circular tour to the walled city, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete, offering views of Niebla's historic fortifications. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla, a moderate 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This route leads through the countryside to an ancient megalithic monument. Local hikers also love the Villarrasa - Gadea Bridge over the Tinto River, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through the unique landscape of the Río Tinto, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Niebla is defined by the unique Río Tinto, ancient Almohad walls, and diverse plains and wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging loops. The routes in Niebla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Niebla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Niebla?
A: Niebla offers a diverse selection of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy strolls, moderate paths, and a few more challenging routes to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What makes hiking in Niebla a unique experience?
A: Hiking in Niebla is unique due to the striking red and orange waters of the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rio_Tinto_(river)" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, which creates an otherworldly landscape. The trails also weave through ancient historical sites, including the impressive Almohad wall encircling the town and the megalithic &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dolmen_de_Soto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural wonder and rich history.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Niebla?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Niebla, offering pleasant temperatures. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dolmen_de_Soto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is particularly interesting during the spring and autumn equinoxes when it aligns with the sunrise, adding a unique historical dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Niebla?
A: The hiking routes in Niebla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique scenery of the Río Tinto and the historical significance of the trails, such as those leading to the ancient walls or the Dolmen de Soto.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Niebla?
A: Yes, Niebla offers several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983673949/fog-circular-tour-to-the-walled-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fog - circular tour to the walled city&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.2-mile (6.8 km) trail providing excellent views of Niebla's historic fortifications. Another moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984106961/dolmen-de-soto-loop-from-niebla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla&lt;/a&gt;, covering 10.8 miles (17.4 km) through the countryside.

Q: Can I hike along the Río Tinto?
A: Absolutely. The Río Tinto is a central feature of the region's hiking landscape. You can enjoy walks along its distinctive red banks, especially near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/386978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge 🌉 Niebla&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984238127/villarrasa-gadea-bridge-over-the-tinto-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrasa - Gadea Bridge over the Tinto River&lt;/a&gt; is another trail that immerses you in this unique environment.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Niebla?
A: Niebla offers several easy trails suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983673949/fog-circular-tour-to-the-walled-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fog - circular tour to the walled city&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice, providing an accessible walk with historical sights. The diverse landscape ensures there are gentle paths for all ages to enjoy.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking near Niebla?
A: Niebla is rich in history. You can hike along the impressive 2-kilometer long &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almohad wall&lt;/a&gt; that encircles the town, offering panoramic views. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/386978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge 🌉 Niebla&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark along the Río Tinto. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984106961/dolmen-de-soto-loop-from-niebla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto&lt;/a&gt;, a megalithic funerary complex, is a must-see historical monument accessible via a moderate loop trail.

Q: Are there any natural attractions besides the Río Tinto?
A: Beyond the iconic Río Tinto, the area around Niebla features diverse natural landscapes. You can explore the plains and wetlands, such as the Pantano de San Walabonso, which offers a different ecological experience. The La Jareta recreation area also provides a natural escape for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Niebla?
A: Yes, Niebla, being a town that welcomes visitors, generally has parking options available, especially near the town center and main attractions. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and designated areas when planning your hike.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay after a hike in Niebla?
A: Niebla offers various options for refreshments and accommodation. The town center has cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can enjoy local cuisine. There are also several accommodation choices, ranging from hotels to guesthouses, to suit different preferences after a day on the trails.
tips:
One of the most important megalithic monuments in Spain that amazes not only for its size but also because its existence has been known for more than 3000 years. You can do a free or guided visit, I suggest you consult the official website to plan your visit:
https://www.juntadeandalucia.es/cultura/agendaculturaldeandalucia/evento/visitas-al-dolmen-de-soto-trigueros

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike, covering approximately 17.4 kilometers, typically takes around 4 hours and 31 minutes to complete. This duration is for active hiking time and does not include stops for breaks or exploring the Dolmen de Soto.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Dolmen de Soto circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate. While the paths are generally accessible, its length of nearly 17.4 kilometers and an elevation gain of about 110 meters mean that a reasonable level of physical fitness is recommended. It's suitable for individuals accustomed to walks of this duration.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail generally starts from the village of Candón, which is located near Niebla. While specific parking details are not provided, as it's a circular route from a village, street parking in Candón would likely be the most common option. Public transport options to Candón or Niebla would need to be researched locally.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily traverses the 'lands of Niebla,' featuring relatively flat terrain interspersed with small hills. You'll follow the Candón stream, indicating a natural environment with water and vegetation. Expect rural, pastoral settings with sections like 'Camino de Las Montuosas' and 'Dehesa Blanca'.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight and namesake of the trail is the Dolmen de Soto. This incredibly significant megalithic monument dates back between 3000 and 2500 BCE and is one of the largest and best-preserved dolmens in the region. It features impressive scale, ancient rock art, and an astronomical alignment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Dolmen de Soto has a unique astronomical alignment, allowing the first rays of sunlight during the spring and autumn equinoxes to illuminate its chamber. Visiting during these times offers a special experience, often with organized programs. Otherwise, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant weather for hiking in this region.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this trail is not available. However, as a rural hiking route, it is generally common for dogs to be allowed, often requiring them to be on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Dolmen de Soto?
faqs.answer: No permits are typically required for hiking the trail itself. However, the Dolmen de Soto, being a significant archaeological site with an interpretation center, may have an entrance fee or specific visiting hours. It's advisable to check their official website for current access information before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Águila and the Camino de Pedro Antón.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Niebla and the village of Candón. Niebla, being a larger town, will offer various facilities including cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. It's a good idea to plan for refreshments before or after your hike in Niebla or Candón, as facilities directly on the trail are unlikely.
